Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] constructed on the western edge of a plateau, with the front mound facing south. Its placement and orientation were likely chosen to make the tumulus visible from Osaka Bay.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/911/00000153|title=Richu Tennō-ryō Kofun / Component Asset / World Heritage|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-22}}</ref>
The mound consists of three tiers and features protrusions (造り出し) on both sides of the constricted area, although the eastern protrusion is now significantly eroded.<ref name=":0" /> The total length of the tumulus is 365 meters, making it the third-largest in Japan after {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Emperor Nintoku's mausoleum) and {{ill|Honmyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honmyōyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Ōjin's mausoleum). The rear mound has a diameter of 205 meters and a height of 27.6 meters, while the front mound measures 235 meters in width and 25.3 meters in height.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html|title=Ranking of Kofun Sizes in Japan|publisher=Sakai City Cultural and Tourism Bureau|accessdate=2021-11-22}}</ref>
Excavations and aerial surveys have revealed the presence of circular platforms at the tops of the front and rear mounds, a unique feature among keyhole-shaped tumuli. These platforms may have held burial facilities.<ref name=":0" />
The tumulus is surrounded by a large moat, with a width of approximately 62 meters at the front mound and 70 meters at the rear mound. While the current moat is single-layered, earlier surveys suggest it originally had an outer moat, possibly a dry moat.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster Research Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|date=2008}}</ref>
Artifacts recovered from the site include a variety of haniwa, such as house-shaped and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as Sue pottery. The presence of a massive quiver-shaped haniwa, standing 1.4 meters tall, suggests the tumulus was constructed in the early 5th century.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster Research Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|date=2008}}</ref>
== Satellite Tumuli == Several smaller tumuli, believed to be satellite tombs (陪塚), surround the main tumulus. Of these, only the {{ill|Shichikannon Kofun|ja|七観音古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichikannon Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Terayama Minamiyama Kofun|ja|寺山南山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terayama Minamiyama Kofun|qq=}} remain intact today. Historical records suggest that other satellite tombs, such as the {{ill|Nanakunoyama Kofun|lt=Nanakunoyama Kofun|lt=Shichikanyama Kofun|ja|七観山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichikanyama Kofun|qq=}}, once existed and were contemporaneous with the main tumulus.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster Research Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|date=2008}}</ref>
== World Heritage == In 2019, Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}}</ref>
== Access == * **JR Hanwa Line**: A 6-minute walk (500 meters) from [[:en:Uenoshiba Station|Uenoshiba Station]] to the Richu Tennō Mausoleum. * **Nankai Koya Line**: From Sakaihigashi Station, take a bus to "Nankyo-dori Yoncho" (南陵通四丁) and walk 5 minutes.
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/017/index.html Mozu Mimihara Minami Mausoleum - Imperial Household Agency] * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/richutenno.html Richu Tennō-ryō Kofun - Sakai City Official Website] * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/index.html Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - World Heritage Site - Sakai City]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun]][[ja:上石津ミサンザイ古墳]][[zh:上石津御陵古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun zenkei-2.JPG|location={{ill|Nishi-ku|WD=Q1042499}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.5539|N|135.4775|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}| Interred_individual = {{ill|Richū|WD=Q329704}}|part_of={{ill|Mozu Tombs|WD=Q4250584}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11359978]] Wikidata name: Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11359978]] Wikidata name: Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun zenkei-2.JPG|280px|thumb|center|Full view of the tumulus (foreground: front mound, background: rear mound)]] '''Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun''' (上石津ミサンザイ古墳), also known as the '''Ishizugaoka Kofun''' (石津ヶ丘古墳), is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Nishi-ku, Sakai|Nishi-ku, Sakai]], Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture. It is situated in the southern part of the [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]] and is one of the largest tumuli in Japan, with a mound length of 365 meters. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has designated it as the "'''Mozu Mimihara Minami Mausoleum'''" (百舌鳥耳原南陵), attributing it to [[:en:Emperor Richū|Emperor Richu]], the 17th emperor of Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Cultural heritage|World Cultural Heritage]] site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."
== Overview == The name "Misanzai" is thought to be a phonetic variation of the term "misasagi" (陵), meaning imperial mausoleum.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://crd.ndl.go.jp/reference/modules/d3ndlcrdentry/index.php?page=ref_view&id=1000261206|title=Etymology of Kofun Names|publisher=National Diet Library|accessdate=2021-11-19}}</ref> Historical maps from the Edo period refer to the site as "Richu Tennō-ryō" (履中天皇陵). Prior to being incorporated into Sakai City, the area was part of {{ill|Shinseki Village|ja|神石村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinseki Village|1=Kamiishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590694}}, Senboku District.
Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] constructed on the western edge of a plateau, with the front mound facing south. Its placement and orientation were likely chosen to make the tumulus visible from Osaka Bay.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/911/00000153|title=Richu Tennō-ryō Kofun / Component Asset / World Heritage|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-22}}</ref>
The mound consists of three tiers and features protrusions (造り出し) on both sides of the constricted area, although the eastern protrusion is now significantly eroded.<ref name=":0" /> The total length of the tumulus is 365 meters, making it the third-largest in Japan after {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Emperor Nintoku's mausoleum) and {{ill|Honmyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honmyōyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Ōjin's mausoleum). The rear mound has a diameter of 205 meters and a height of 27.6 meters, while the front mound measures 235 meters in width and 25.3 meters in height.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html|title=Ranking of Kofun Sizes in Japan|publisher=Sakai City Cultural and Tourism Bureau|accessdate=2021-11-22}}</ref>
Excavations and aerial surveys have revealed the presence of circular platforms at the tops of the front and rear mounds, a unique feature among keyhole-shaped tumuli. These platforms may have held burial facilities.<ref name=":0" />
The tumulus is surrounded by a large moat, with a width of approximately 62 meters at the front mound and 70 meters at the rear mound. While the current moat is single-layered, earlier surveys suggest it originally had an outer moat, possibly a dry moat.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster Research Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|date=2008}}</ref>
Artifacts recovered from the site include a variety of haniwa, such as house-shaped and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as Sue pottery. The presence of a massive quiver-shaped haniwa, standing 1.4 meters tall, suggests the tumulus was constructed in the early 5th century.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster Research Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|date=2008}}</ref>
== Satellite Tumuli == Several smaller tumuli, believed to be satellite tombs (陪塚), surround the main tumulus. Of these, only the {{ill|Shichikannon Kofun|ja|七観音古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichikannon Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Terayama Minamiyama Kofun|ja|寺山南山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terayama Minamiyama Kofun|qq=}} remain intact today. Historical records suggest that other satellite tombs, such as the {{ill|Nanakunoyama Kofun|lt=Nanakunoyama Kofun|lt=Shichikanyama Kofun|ja|七観山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichikanyama Kofun|qq=}}, once existed and were contemporaneous with the main tumulus.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster Research Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|date=2008}}</ref>
== World Heritage == In 2019, Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}}</ref>
== Access == * **JR Hanwa Line**: A 6-minute walk (500 meters) from [[:en:Uenoshiba Station|Uenoshiba Station]] to the Richu Tennō Mausoleum. * **Nankai Koya Line**: From Sakaihigashi Station, take a bus to "Nankyo-dori Yoncho" (南陵通四丁) and walk 5 minutes.

Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] constructed on the western edge of a plateau, with the front mound facing south. Its placement and orientation were likely chosen to make the tumulus visible from Osaka Bay.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/911/00000153|title=Richu Tennō-ryō Kofun / Component Asset / World Heritage|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-22}}</ref>
The mound consists of three tiers and features protrusions (造り出し) on both sides of the constricted area, although the eastern protrusion is now significantly eroded.<ref name=":0" /> The total length of the tumulus is 365 meters, making it the third-largest in Japan after {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Emperor Nintoku's mausoleum) and {{ill|Honmyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honmyōyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Ōjin's mausoleum). The rear mound has a diameter of 205 meters and a height of 27.6 meters, while the front mound measures 235 meters in width and 25.3 meters in height.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html|title=Ranking of Kofun Sizes in Japan|publisher=Sakai City Cultural and Tourism Bureau|accessdate=2021-11-22}}</ref>
Excavations and aerial surveys have revealed the presence of circular platforms at the tops of the front and rear mounds, a unique feature among keyhole-shaped tumuli. These platforms may have held burial facilities.<ref name=":0" />
The tumulus is surrounded by a large moat, with a width of approximately 62 meters at the front mound and 70 meters at the rear mound. While the current moat is single-layered, earlier surveys suggest it originally had an outer moat, possibly a dry moat.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster Research Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|date=2008}}</ref>
Artifacts recovered from the site include a variety of haniwa, such as house-shaped and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as Sue pottery. The presence of a massive quiver-shaped haniwa, standing 1.4 meters tall, suggests the tumulus was constructed in the early 5th century.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster Research Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|date=2008}}</ref>
== Satellite Tumuli == Several smaller tumuli, believed to be satellite tombs (陪塚), surround the main tumulus. Of these, only the {{ill|Shichikannon Kofun|ja|七観音古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichikannon Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Terayama Minamiyama Kofun|ja|寺山南山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terayama Minamiyama Kofun|qq=}} remain intact today. Historical records suggest that other satellite tombs, such as the {{ill|Nanakunoyama Kofun|lt=Nanakunoyama Kofun|lt=Shichikanyama Kofun|ja|七観山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichikanyama Kofun|qq=}}, once existed and were contemporaneous with the main tumulus.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster Research Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|date=2008}}</ref>
== World Heritage == In 2019, Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}}</ref>
== Access == * **JR Hanwa Line**: A 6-minute walk (500 meters) from [[:en:Uenoshiba Station|Uenoshiba Station]] to the Richu Tennō Mausoleum. * **Nankai Koya Line**: From Sakaihigashi Station, take a bus to "Nankyo-dori Yoncho" (南陵通四丁) and walk 5 minutes.
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/017/index.html Mozu Mimihara Minami Mausoleum - Imperial Household Agency] * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/richutenno.html Richu Tennō-ryō Kofun - Sakai City Official Website] * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/index.html Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - World Heritage Site - Sakai City]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun]][[ja:上石津ミサンザイ古墳]][[zh:上石津御陵古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun zenkei-2.JPG|location={{ill|Nishi-ku|WD=Q1042499}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.5539|N|135.4775|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}| Interred_individual = {{ill|Richū|WD=Q329704}}|part_of={{ill|Mozu Tombs|WD=Q4250584}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11359978]] Wikidata name: Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11359978]] Wikidata name: Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun zenkei-2.JPG|280px|thumb|center|Full view of the tumulus (foreground: front mound, background: rear mound)]] '''Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun''' (上石津ミサンザイ古墳), also known as the '''Ishizugaoka Kofun''' (石津ヶ丘古墳), is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Nishi-ku, Sakai|Nishi-ku, Sakai]], Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture. It is situated in the southern part of the [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]] and is one of the largest tumuli in Japan, with a mound length of 365 meters. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has designated it as the "'''Mozu Mimihara Minami Mausoleum'''" (百舌鳥耳原南陵), attributing it to [[:en:Emperor Richū|Emperor Richu]], the 17th emperor of Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Cultural heritage|World Cultural Heritage]] site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."
== Overview == The name "Misanzai" is thought to be a phonetic variation of the term "misasagi" (陵), meaning imperial mausoleum.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://crd.ndl.go.jp/reference/modules/d3ndlcrdentry/index.php?page=ref_view&id=1000261206|title=Etymology of Kofun Names|publisher=National Diet Library|accessdate=2021-11-19}}</ref> Historical maps from the Edo period refer to the site as "Richu Tennō-ryō" (履中天皇陵). Prior to being incorporated into Sakai City, the area was part of {{ill|Shinseki Village|ja|神石村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinseki Village|1=Kamiishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590694}}, Senboku District.
Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] constructed on the western edge of a plateau, with the front mound facing south. The rear mound has a diameter of 205 meters and a height of 27.6 meters, while the front mound measures 235 meters in width and 25.3 meters in height.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html|title=Ranking of Kofun Sizes in Japan|publisher=Sakai City Cultural and Tourism Bureau|accessdate=2021-11-22}}</ref>
Excavations and aerial surveys have revealed the presence of circular platforms at the tops of the front and rear mounds, a unique feature among keyhole-shaped tumuli. These platforms may have held burial facilities.<ref name=":0" />
The tumulus is surrounded by a large moat, with a width of approximately 62 meters at the front mound and 70 meters at the rear mound. While the current moat is single-layered, earlier surveys suggest it originally had an outer moat, possibly a dry moat.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster Research Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|date=2008}}</ref>
Artifacts recovered from the site include a variety of haniwa, such as house-shaped and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as Sue pottery. The presence of a massive quiver-shaped haniwa, standing 1.4 meters tall, suggests the tumulus was constructed in the early 5th century.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster Research Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|date=2008}}</ref>
== Satellite Tumuli == Several smaller tumuli, believed to be satellite tombs (陪塚), surround the main tumulus. Of these, only the {{ill|Shichikannon Kofun|ja|七観音古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichikannon Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Terayama Minamiyama Kofun|ja|寺山南山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terayama Minamiyama Kofun|qq=}} remain intact today. Historical records suggest that other satellite tombs, such as the {{ill|Nanakunoyama Kofun|lt=Nanakunoyama Kofun|lt=Shichikanyama Kofun|ja|七観山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichikanyama Kofun|qq=}}, once existed and were contemporaneous with the main tumulus.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster Research Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|date=2008}}</ref>

== Access == * **JR Hanwa Line**: A 6-minute walk (500 meters) from [[:en:Uenoshiba Station|Uenoshiba Station]] to the Richu Tennō Mausoleum. * **Nankai Koya Line**: From Sakaihigashi Station, take a bus to "Nankyo-dori Yoncho" (南陵通四丁) and walk 5 minutes.

Prior to being incorporated into Sakai City, the area was part of {{ill|Shinseki Village|ja|神石村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinseki Village|1=Kamiishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590694}}, Senboku District.
Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] constructed on the western edge of a plateau, with the front mound facing south. Its placement and orientation were likely chosen to make the tumulus visible from Osaka Bay.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/911/00000153|title=Richu Tennō-ryō Kofun / Component Asset / World Heritage|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-22}}</ref>
The mound consists of three tiers and features protrusions (造り出し) on both sides of the constricted area, although the eastern protrusion is now significantly eroded.<ref name=":0" /> The total length of the tumulus is 365 meters, making it the third-largest in Japan after {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Emperor Nintoku's mausoleum) and {{ill|Honmyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honmyōyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Ōjin's mausoleum). The rear mound has a diameter of 205 meters and a height of 27.6 meters, while the front mound measures 235 meters in width and 25.3 meters in height.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html|title=Ranking of Kofun Sizes in Japan|publisher=Sakai City Cultural and Tourism Bureau|accessdate=2021-11-22}}</ref>
Excavations and aerial surveys have revealed the presence of circular platforms at the tops of the front and rear mounds, a unique feature among keyhole-shaped tumuli. These platforms may have held burial facilities.<ref name=":0" />
The tumulus is surrounded by a large moat, with a width of approximately 62 meters at the front mound and 70 meters at the rear mound. While the current moat is single-layered, earlier surveys suggest it originally had an outer moat, possibly a dry moat.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster Research Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|date=2008}}</ref>
Artifacts recovered from the site include a variety of haniwa, such as house-shaped and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as Sue pottery. The presence of a massive quiver-shaped haniwa, standing 1.4 meters tall, suggests the tumulus was constructed in the early 5th century.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster Research Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|date=2008}}</ref>
== Satellite Tumuli == Several smaller tumuli, believed to be satellite tombs (陪塚), surround the main tumulus. Of these, only the {{ill|Shichikannon Kofun|ja|七観音古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichikannon Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Terayama Minamiyama Kofun|ja|寺山南山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terayama Minamiyama Kofun|qq=}} remain intact today. Historical records suggest that other satellite tombs, such as the {{ill|Nanakunoyama Kofun|lt=Nanakunoyama Kofun|lt=Shichikanyama Kofun|ja|七観山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichikanyama Kofun|qq=}}, once existed and were contemporaneous with the main tumulus.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster Research Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|date=2008}}</ref>
== World Heritage == In 2019, Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}}</ref>
== Access == * **JR Hanwa Line**: A 6-minute walk (500 meters) from [[:en:Uenoshiba Station|Uenoshiba Station]] to the Richu Tennō Mausoleum. * **Nankai Koya Line**: From Sakaihigashi Station, take a bus to "Nankyo-dori Yoncho" (南陵通四丁) and walk 5 minutes.

The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has designated it as the "'''Mozu Mimihara Minami Mausoleum'''" (百舌鳥耳原南陵), attributing it to [[:en:Emperor Richū|Emperor Richu]], the 17th emperor of Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Cultural heritage|World Cultural Heritage]] site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."
== Overview == The name "Misanzai" is thought to be a phonetic variation of the term "misasagi" (陵), meaning imperial mausoleum.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://crd.ndl.go.jp/reference/modules/d3ndlcrdentry/index.php?page=ref_view&id=1000261206|title=Etymology of Kofun Names|publisher=National Diet Library|accessdate=2021-11-19}}</ref> Historical maps from the Edo period refer to the site as "Richu Tennō-ryō" (履中天皇陵). Prior to being incorporated into Sakai City, the area was part of {{ill|Shinseki Village|ja|神石村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinseki Village|1=Kamiishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590694}}, Senboku District.
Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] constructed on the western edge of a plateau, with the front mound facing south. Its placement and orientation were likely chosen to make the tumulus visible from Osaka Bay.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/911/00000153|title=Richu Tennō-ryō Kofun / Component Asset / World Heritage|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-22}}</ref>
The mound consists of three tiers and features protrusions (造り出し) on both sides of the constricted area, although the eastern protrusion is now significantly eroded.<ref name=":0" /> The total length of the tumulus is 365 meters, making it the third-largest in Japan after {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Emperor Nintoku's mausoleum) and {{ill|Honmyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honmyōyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Ōjin's mausoleum). The rear mound has a diameter of 205 meters and a height of 27.6 meters, while the front mound measures 235 meters in width and 25.3 meters in height.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html|title=Ranking of Kofun Sizes in Japan|publisher=Sakai City Cultural and Tourism Bureau|accessdate=2021-11-22}}</ref>
Excavations and aerial surveys have revealed the presence of circular platforms at the tops of the front and rear mounds, a unique feature among keyhole-shaped tumuli. These platforms may have held burial facilities.<ref name=":0" />
The tumulus is surrounded by a large moat, with a width of approximately 62 meters at the front mound and 70 meters at the rear mound. While the current moat is single-layered, earlier surveys suggest it originally had an outer moat, possibly a dry moat.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster Research Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|date=2008}}</ref>
Artifacts recovered from the site include a variety of haniwa, such as house-shaped and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as Sue pottery. The presence of a massive quiver-shaped haniwa, standing 1.4 meters tall, suggests the tumulus was constructed in the early 5th century.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster Research Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|date=2008}}</ref>
== Satellite Tumuli == Several smaller tumuli, believed to be satellite tombs (陪塚), surround the main tumulus. Of these, only the {{ill|Shichikannon Kofun|ja|七観音古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichikannon Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Terayama Minamiyama Kofun|ja|寺山南山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terayama Minamiyama Kofun|qq=}} remain intact today. Historical records suggest that other satellite tombs, such as the {{ill|Nanakunoyama Kofun|lt=Nanakunoyama Kofun|lt=Shichikanyama Kofun|ja|七観山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichikanyama Kofun|qq=}}, once existed and were contemporaneous with the main tumulus.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster Research Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|date=2008}}</ref>
== World Heritage == In 2019, Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}}</ref>
== Access == * **JR Hanwa Line**: A 6-minute walk (500 meters) from [[:en:Uenoshiba Station|Uenoshiba Station]] to the Richu Tennō Mausoleum. * **Nankai Koya Line**: From Sakaihigashi Station, take a bus to "Nankyo-dori Yoncho" (南陵通四丁) and walk 5 minutes.
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/017/index.html Mozu Mimihara Minami Mausoleum - Imperial Household Agency] * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/richutenno.html Richu Tennō-ryō Kofun - Sakai City Official Website] * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/index.html Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - World Heritage Site - Sakai City]

[[ja:上石津ミサンザイ古墳]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun}} [[zh:上石津御陵古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11359978}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Nishi-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun]][[ja:上石津ミサンザイ古墳]][[zh:上石津御陵古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun]][[ja:上石津ミサンザイ古墳]][[zh:上石津御陵古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun]][[ja:上石津ミサンザイ古墳]][[zh:上石津御陵古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun]][[ja:上石津ミサンザイ古墳]][[zh:上石津御陵古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun zenkei-2.JPG|location={{ill|Nishi-ku|WD=Q1042499}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.5539|N|135.4775|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}| Interred_individual = {{ill|Richū|WD=Q329704}}|part_of={{ill|Mozu Tombs|WD=Q4250584}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11359978]] Wikidata name: Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11359978]] Wikidata name: Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun zenkei-2.JPG|280px|thumb|center|Full view of the tumulus (foreground: front mound, background: rear mound)]] '''Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun''' (上石津ミサンザイ古墳), also known as the '''Ishizugaoka Kofun''' (石津ヶ丘古墳), is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Nishi-ku, Sakai|Nishi-ku, Sakai]], Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture. It is situated in the southern part of the [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]] and is one of the largest tumuli in Japan, with a mound length of 365 meters. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has designated it as the "'''Mozu Mimihara Minami Mausoleum'''" (百舌鳥耳原南陵), attributing it to [[:en:Emperor Richū|Emperor Richu]], the 17th emperor of Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Cultural heritage|World Cultural Heritage]] site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."
== Overview == The name "Misanzai" is thought to be a phonetic variation of the term "misasagi" (陵), meaning imperial mausoleum.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://crd.ndl.go.jp/reference/modules/d3ndlcrdentry/index.php?page=ref_view&id=1000261206|title=Etymology of Kofun Names|publisher=National Diet Library|accessdate=2021-11-19}}</ref> Historical maps from the Edo period refer to the site as "Richu Tennō-ryō" (履中天皇陵). Prior to being incorporated into Sakai City, the area was part of {{ill|Shinseki Village|ja|神石村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinseki Village|1=Kamiishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590694}}, Senboku District.
Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] constructed on the western edge of a plateau, with the front mound facing south. Its placement and orientation were likely chosen to make the tumulus visible from Osaka Bay.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/911/00000153|title=Richu Tennō-ryō Kofun / Component Asset / World Heritage|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-22}}</ref>
The mound consists of three tiers and features protrusions (造り出し) on both sides of the constricted area, although the eastern protrusion is now significantly eroded.<ref name=":0" /> The total length of the tumulus is 365 meters, making it the third-largest in Japan after {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Emperor Nintoku's mausoleum) and {{ill|Honmyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honmyōyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Ōjin's mausoleum). The rear mound has a diameter of 205 meters and a height of 27.6 meters, while the front mound measures 235 meters in width and 25.3 meters in height.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html|title=Ranking of Kofun Sizes in Japan|publisher=Sakai City Cultural and Tourism Bureau|accessdate=2021-11-22}}</ref>
Excavations and aerial surveys have revealed the presence of circular platforms at the tops of the front and rear mounds, a unique feature among keyhole-shaped tumuli. These platforms may have held burial facilities.<ref name=":0" />
The tumulus is surrounded by a large moat, with a width of approximately 62 meters at the front mound and 70 meters at the rear mound. While the current moat is single-layered, earlier surveys suggest it originally had an outer moat, possibly a dry moat.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster Research Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|date=2008}}</ref>
Artifacts recovered from the site include a variety of haniwa, such as house-shaped and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as Sue pottery. The presence of a massive quiver-shaped haniwa, standing 1.4 meters tall, suggests the tumulus was constructed in the early 5th century.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster Research Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|date=2008}}</ref>
== Satellite Tumuli == Several smaller tumuli, believed to be satellite tombs (陪塚), surround the main tumulus. Of these, only the {{ill|Shichikannon Kofun|ja|七観音古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichikannon Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Terayama Minamiyama Kofun|ja|寺山南山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terayama Minamiyama Kofun|qq=}} remain intact today. Historical records suggest that other satellite tombs, such as the {{ill|Nanakunoyama Kofun|lt=Nanakunoyama Kofun|lt=Shichikanyama Kofun|ja|七観山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichikanyama Kofun|qq=}}, once existed and were contemporaneous with the main tumulus.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster Research Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|date=2008}}</ref>
== World Heritage == In 2019, Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}}</ref>
== Access == * **JR Hanwa Line**: A 6-minute walk (500 meters) from [[:en:Uenoshiba Station|Uenoshiba Station]] to the Richu Tennō Mausoleum. * **Nankai Koya Line**: From Sakaihigashi Station, take a bus to "Nankyo-dori Yoncho" (南陵通四丁) and walk 5 minutes.

== Access == * **JR Hanwa Line**: A 6-minute walk (500 meters) from [[:en:Uenoshiba Station|Uenoshiba Station]] to the Richu Tennō Mausoleum. * **Nankai Koya Line**: From Sakaihigashi Station, take a bus to "Nankyo-dori Yoncho" (南陵通四丁) and walk 5 minutes.

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun]][[ja:上石津ミサンザイ古墳]][[zh:上石津御陵古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun]][[ja:上石津ミサンザイ古墳]][[zh:上石津御陵古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun]][[ja:上石津ミサンザイ古墳]][[zh:上石津御陵古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun]][[ja:上石津ミサンザイ古墳]][[zh:上石津御陵古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun zenkei-2.JPG|location={{ill|Nishi-ku|WD=Q1042499}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.5539|N|135.4775|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}| Interred_individual = {{ill|Richū|WD=Q329704}}|part_of={{ill|Mozu Tombs|WD=Q4250584}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11359978]] Wikidata name: Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11359978]] Wikidata name: Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun zenkei-2.JPG|280px|thumb|center|Full view of the tumulus (foreground: front mound, background: rear mound)]] '''Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun''' (上石津ミサンザイ古墳), also known as the '''Ishizugaoka Kofun''' (石津ヶ丘古墳), is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|en|Nishi-ku, Sakai|WD=Q1042499|lt=Nishi-ku, Sakai}}, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture. It is situated in the southern part of the {{ill|Mozu Tombs|en|Mozu Tombs|WD=Q4250584|lt=Mozu Tombs}} and is one of the largest tumuli in Japan, with a mound length of 365 meters. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} has designated it as the "'''Mozu Mimihara Minami Mausoleum'''" (百舌鳥耳原南陵), attributing it to {{ill|Emperor Richū|en|Emperor Richū|WD=Q329704|lt=Emperor Richu}}, the 17th emperor of Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Cultural heritage|en|Cultural heritage|WD=Q210272|lt=World Cultural Heritage}} site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."
== Overview == The name "Misanzai" is thought to be a phonetic variation of the term "misasagi" (陵), meaning imperial mausoleum.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://crd.ndl.go.jp/reference/modules/d3ndlcrdentry/index.php?page=ref_view&id=1000261206|title=Etymology of Kofun Names|publisher=National Diet Library|accessdate=2021-11-19}}</ref> Historical maps from the Edo period refer to the site as "Richu Tennō-ryō" (履中天皇陵). Prior to being incorporated into Sakai City, the area was part of {{ill|Shinseki Village|ja|神石村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinseki Village|1=Kamiishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590694}}, Senboku District.
Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} constructed on the western edge of a plateau, with the front mound facing south. Its placement and orientation were likely chosen to make the tumulus visible from Osaka Bay.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/911/00000153|title=Richu Tennō-ryō Kofun / Component Asset / World Heritage|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-22}}</ref>
The mound consists of three tiers and features protrusions (造り出し) on both sides of the constricted area, although the eastern protrusion is now significantly eroded.<ref name=":0" /> The total length of the tumulus is 365 meters, making it the third-largest in Japan after {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Emperor Nintoku's mausoleum) and {{ill|Honmyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honmyōyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Ōjin's mausoleum). The rear mound has a diameter of 205 meters and a height of 27.6 meters, while the front mound measures 235 meters in width and 25.3 meters in height.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html|title=Ranking of Kofun Sizes in Japan|publisher=Sakai City Cultural and Tourism Bureau|accessdate=2021-11-22}}</ref>
Excavations and aerial surveys have revealed the presence of circular platforms at the tops of the front and rear mounds, a unique feature among keyhole-shaped tumuli. These platforms may have held burial facilities.<ref name=":0" />
The tumulus is surrounded by a large moat, with a width of approximately 62 meters at the front mound and 70 meters at the rear mound. While the current moat is single-layered, earlier surveys suggest it originally had an outer moat, possibly a dry moat.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster Research Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|date=2008}}</ref>
Artifacts recovered from the site include a variety of haniwa, such as house-shaped and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as Sue pottery. The presence of a massive quiver-shaped haniwa, standing 1.4 meters tall, suggests the tumulus was constructed in the early 5th century.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster Research Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|date=2008}}</ref>
== Satellite Tumuli == Several smaller tumuli, believed to be satellite tombs (陪塚), surround the main tumulus. Of these, only the {{ill|Shichikannon Kofun|ja|七観音古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichikannon Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Terayama Minamiyama Kofun|ja|寺山南山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terayama Minamiyama Kofun|qq=}} remain intact today. Historical records suggest that other satellite tombs, such as the {{ill|Nanakunoyama Kofun|lt=Nanakunoyama Kofun|lt=Shichikanyama Kofun|ja|七観山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichikanyama Kofun|qq=}}, once existed and were contemporaneous with the main tumulus.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster Research Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|date=2008}}</ref>
== World Heritage == In 2019, Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}}</ref>
== Access == * **JR Hanwa Line**: A 6-minute walk (500 meters) from {{ill|Uenoshiba Station|en|Uenoshiba Station|WD=Q856750|lt=Uenoshiba Station}} to the Richu Tennō Mausoleum. * **Nankai Koya Line**: From Sakaihigashi Station, take a bus to "Nankyo-dori Yoncho" (南陵通四丁) and walk 5 minutes.
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/017/index.html Mozu Mimihara Minami Mausoleum - Imperial Household Agency] * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/richutenno.html Richu Tennō-ryō Kofun - Sakai City Official Website] * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/index.html Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - World Heritage Site - Sakai City] {{translated page|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|version=103389543|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} constructed on the western edge of a plateau, with the front mound facing south. Its placement and orientation were likely chosen to make the tumulus visible from Osaka Bay.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/911/00000153|title=Richu Tennō-ryō Kofun / Component Asset / World Heritage|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-22}}</ref>
The mound consists of three tiers and features protrusions (造り出し) on both sides of the constricted area, although the eastern protrusion is now significantly eroded.<ref name=":0" /> The total length of the tumulus is 365 meters, making it the third-largest in Japan after {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Emperor Nintoku's mausoleum) and {{ill|Honmyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honmyōyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Ōjin's mausoleum). The rear mound has a diameter of 205 meters and a height of 27.6 meters, while the front mound measures 235 meters in width and 25.3 meters in height.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html|title=Ranking of Kofun Sizes in Japan|publisher=Sakai City Cultural and Tourism Bureau|accessdate=2021-11-22}}</ref>
Excavations and aerial surveys have revealed the presence of circular platforms at the tops of the front and rear mounds, a unique feature among keyhole-shaped tumuli. These platforms may have held burial facilities.<ref name=":0" />
The tumulus is surrounded by a large moat, with a width of approximately 62 meters at the front mound and 70 meters at the rear mound. While the current moat is single-layered, earlier surveys suggest it originally had an outer moat, possibly a dry moat.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster Research Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|date=2008}}</ref>
Artifacts recovered from the site include a variety of haniwa, such as house-shaped and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as Sue pottery. The presence of a massive quiver-shaped haniwa, standing 1.4 meters tall, suggests the tumulus was constructed in the early 5th century.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster Research Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|date=2008}}</ref>
== Satellite Tumuli == Several smaller tumuli, believed to be satellite tombs (陪塚), surround the main tumulus. Of these, only the {{ill|Shichikannon Kofun|ja|七観音古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichikannon Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Terayama Minamiyama Kofun|ja|寺山南山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terayama Minamiyama Kofun|qq=}} remain intact today. Historical records suggest that other satellite tombs, such as the {{ill|Nanakunoyama Kofun|lt=Nanakunoyama Kofun|lt=Shichikanyama Kofun|ja|七観山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichikanyama Kofun|qq=}}, once existed and were contemporaneous with the main tumulus.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster Research Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|date=2008}}</ref>
== World Heritage == In 2019, Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}}</ref>
== Access == * **JR Hanwa Line**: A 6-minute walk (500 meters) from {{ill|Uenoshiba Station|en|Uenoshiba Station|WD=Q856750|lt=Uenoshiba Station}} to the Richu Tennō Mausoleum. * **Nankai Koya Line**: From Sakaihigashi Station, take a bus to "Nankyo-dori Yoncho" (南陵通四丁) and walk 5 minutes.
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/017/index.html Mozu Mimihara Minami Mausoleum - Imperial Household Agency] * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/richutenno.html Richu Tennō-ryō Kofun - Sakai City Official Website] * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/index.html Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - World Heritage Site - Sakai City] {{translated page|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|version=103389543|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun]][[ja:上石津ミサンザイ古墳]][[zh:上石津御陵古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun]][[ja:上石津ミサンザイ古墳]][[zh:上石津御陵古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun]][[ja:上石津ミサンザイ古墳]][[zh:上石津御陵古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun]][[ja:上石津ミサンザイ古墳]][[zh:上石津御陵古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun zenkei-2.JPG|location={{ill|Nishi-ku|WD=Q1042499}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.5539|N|135.4775|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}| Interred_individual = {{ill|Richū|WD=Q329704}}|part_of={{ill|Mozu Tombs|WD=Q4250584}}}}
[[File:Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun zenkei-2.JPG|280px|thumb|center|Full view of the tumulus (foreground: front mound, background: rear mound)]] '''Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun''' (上石津ミサンザイ古墳), also known as the '''Ishizugaoka Kofun''' (石津ヶ丘古墳), is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|en|Nishi-ku, Sakai|WD=Q1042499|lt=Nishi-ku, Sakai}}, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture. It is situated in the southern part of the {{ill|Mozu Tombs|en|Mozu Tombs|WD=Q4250584|lt=Mozu Tombs}} and is one of the largest tumuli in Japan, with a mound length of 365 meters. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} has designated it as the "'''Mozu Mimihara Minami Mausoleum'''" (百舌鳥耳原南陵), attributing it to {{ill|Emperor Richū|en|Emperor Richū|WD=Q329704|lt=Emperor Richu}}, the 17th emperor of Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Cultural heritage|en|Cultural heritage|WD=Q210272|lt=World Cultural Heritage}} site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."
== Overview == The name "Misanzai" is thought to be a phonetic variation of the term "misasagi" (陵), meaning imperial mausoleum.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://crd.ndl.go.jp/reference/modules/d3ndlcrdentry/index.php?page=ref_view&id=1000261206|title=Etymology of Kofun Names|publisher=National Diet Library|accessdate=2021-11-19}}</ref> Historical maps from the Edo period refer to the site as "Richu Tennō-ryō" (履中天皇陵). Prior to being incorporated into Sakai City, the area was part of {{ill|Shinseki Village|ja|神石村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinseki Village|1=Kamiishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590694}}, Senboku District.
Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} constructed on the western edge of a plateau, with the front mound facing south. Its placement and orientation were likely chosen to make the tumulus visible from Osaka Bay.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/911/00000153|title=Richu Tennō-ryō Kofun / Component Asset / World Heritage|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-22}}</ref>
The mound consists of three tiers and features protrusions (造り出し) on both sides of the constricted area, although the eastern protrusion is now significantly eroded.<ref name=":0" /> The total length of the tumulus is 365 meters, making it the third-largest in Japan after {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Emperor Nintoku's mausoleum) and {{ill|Honmyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honmyōyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Ōjin's mausoleum). The rear mound has a diameter of 205 meters and a height of 27.6 meters, while the front mound measures 235 meters in width and 25.3 meters in height.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html|title=Ranking of Kofun Sizes in Japan|publisher=Sakai City Cultural and Tourism Bureau|accessdate=2021-11-22}}</ref>
Excavations and aerial surveys have revealed the presence of circular platforms at the tops of the front and rear mounds, a unique feature among keyhole-shaped tumuli. These platforms may have held burial facilities.<ref name=":0" />
The tumulus is surrounded by a large moat, with a width of approximately 62 meters at the front mound and 70 meters at the rear mound. While the current moat is single-layered, earlier surveys suggest it originally had an outer moat, possibly a dry moat.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster Research Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|date=2008}}</ref>
Artifacts recovered from the site include a variety of haniwa, such as house-shaped and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as Sue pottery. The presence of a massive quiver-shaped haniwa, standing 1.4 meters tall, suggests the tumulus was constructed in the early 5th century.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster Research Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|date=2008}}</ref>
== Satellite Tumuli == Several smaller tumuli, believed to be satellite tombs (陪塚), surround the main tumulus. Of these, only the {{ill|Shichikannon Kofun|ja|七観音古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichikannon Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Terayama Minamiyama Kofun|ja|寺山南山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terayama Minamiyama Kofun|qq=}} remain intact today. Historical records suggest that other satellite tombs, such as the {{ill|Nanakunoyama Kofun|lt=Nanakunoyama Kofun|lt=Shichikanyama Kofun|ja|七観山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichikanyama Kofun|qq=}}, once existed and were contemporaneous with the main tumulus.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster Research Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|date=2008}}</ref>
== World Heritage == In 2019, Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}}</ref>
== Access == * **JR Hanwa Line**: A 6-minute walk (500 meters) from {{ill|Uenoshiba Station|en|Uenoshiba Station|WD=Q856750|lt=Uenoshiba Station}} to the Richu Tennō Mausoleum. * **Nankai Koya Line**: From Sakaihigashi Station, take a bus to "Nankyo-dori Yoncho" (南陵通四丁) and walk 5 minutes.

[[File:Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun zenkei-2.JPG|280px|thumb|center|Full view of the tumulus (foreground: front mound, background: rear mound)]] '''Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun''' (上石津ミサンザイ古墳), also known as the '''Ishizugaoka Kofun''' (石津ヶ丘古墳), is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|en|Nishi-ku, Sakai|WD=Q1042499|lt=Nishi-ku, Sakai|1=Nishi-ku, Sakai}}, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture. It is situated in the southern part of the {{ill|Mozu Tombs|en|Mozu Tombs|WD=Q4250584|lt=Mozu Tombs|1=Mozu Tombs}} and is one of the largest tumuli in Japan, with a mound length of 365 meters. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} has designated it as the "'''Mozu Mimihara Minami Mausoleum'''" (百舌鳥耳原南陵), attributing it to {{ill|Emperor Richū|en|Emperor Richū|WD=Q329704|lt=Emperor Richu|1=Emperor Richū}}, the 17th emperor of Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Cultural heritage|en|Cultural heritage|WD=Q210272|lt=World Cultural Heritage|1=Cultural heritage}} site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."
== Overview == The name "Misanzai" is thought to be a phonetic variation of the term "misasagi" (陵), meaning imperial mausoleum.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://crd.ndl.go.jp/reference/modules/d3ndlcrdentry/index.php?page=ref_view&id=1000261206|title=Etymology of Kofun Names|publisher=National Diet Library|accessdate=2021-11-19}}</ref> Historical maps from the Edo period refer to the site as "Richu Tennō-ryō" (履中天皇陵). Prior to being incorporated into Sakai City, the area was part of {{ill|Shinseki Village|ja|神石村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinseki Village|1=Kamiishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590694}}, Senboku District.
Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} constructed on the western edge of a plateau, with the front mound facing south. Its placement and orientation were likely chosen to make the tumulus visible from Osaka Bay.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/911/00000153|title=Richu Tennō-ryō Kofun / Component Asset / World Heritage|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-22}}</ref>
The mound consists of three tiers and features protrusions (造り出し) on both sides of the constricted area, although the eastern protrusion is now significantly eroded.<ref name=":0" /> The total length of the tumulus is 365 meters, making it the third-largest in Japan after {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Emperor Nintoku's mausoleum) and {{ill|Honmyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honmyōyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Ōjin's mausoleum). The rear mound has a diameter of 205 meters and a height of 27.6 meters, while the front mound measures 235 meters in width and 25.3 meters in height.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html|title=Ranking of Kofun Sizes in Japan|publisher=Sakai City Cultural and Tourism Bureau|accessdate=2021-11-22}}</ref>
Excavations and aerial surveys have revealed the presence of circular platforms at the tops of the front and rear mounds, a unique feature among keyhole-shaped tumuli. These platforms may have held burial facilities.<ref name=":0" />
The tumulus is surrounded by a large moat, with a width of approximately 62 meters at the front mound and 70 meters at the rear mound. While the current moat is single-layered, earlier surveys suggest it originally had an outer moat, possibly a dry moat.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster Research Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|date=2008}}</ref>
Artifacts recovered from the site include a variety of haniwa, such as house-shaped and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as Sue pottery. The presence of a massive quiver-shaped haniwa, standing 1.4 meters tall, suggests the tumulus was constructed in the early 5th century.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster Research Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|date=2008}}</ref>
== Satellite Tumuli == Several smaller tumuli, believed to be satellite tombs (陪塚), surround the main tumulus. Of these, only the {{ill|Shichikannon Kofun|ja|七観音古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichikannon Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Terayama Minamiyama Kofun|ja|寺山南山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terayama Minamiyama Kofun|qq=}} remain intact today. Historical records suggest that other satellite tombs, such as the {{ill|Nanakunoyama Kofun|lt=Nanakunoyama Kofun|lt=Shichikanyama Kofun|ja|七観山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichikanyama Kofun|qq=}}, once existed and were contemporaneous with the main tumulus.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster Research Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|date=2008}}</ref>
== World Heritage == In 2019, Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}}</ref>
== Access == * **JR Hanwa Line**: A 6-minute walk (500 meters) from {{ill|Uenoshiba Station|en|Uenoshiba Station|WD=Q856750|lt=Uenoshiba Station|1=Uenoshiba Station}} to the Richu Tennō Mausoleum. * **Nankai Koya Line**: From Sakaihigashi Station, take a bus to "Nankyo-dori Yoncho" (南陵通四丁) and walk 5 minutes.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun]][[ja:上石津ミサンザイ古墳]][[zh:上石津御陵古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun]][[ja:上石津ミサンザイ古墳]][[zh:上石津御陵古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun]][[ja:上石津ミサンザイ古墳]][[zh:上石津御陵古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun]][[ja:上石津ミサンザイ古墳]][[zh:上石津御陵古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun zenkei-2.JPG|location={{ill|Nishi-ku|WD=Q1042499|1=Nishi-ku, Sakai}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.5539|N|135.4775|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}| Interred_individual = {{ill|Richū|WD=Q329704|1=Emperor Richū}}|part_of={{ill|Mozu Tombs|WD=Q4250584|1=Mozu Tombs}}}}
[[File:Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun zenkei-2.JPG|280px|thumb|center|Full view of the tumulus (foreground: front mound, background: rear mound)]] '''Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun''' (上石津ミサンザイ古墳), also known as the '''Ishizugaoka Kofun''' (石津ヶ丘古墳), is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|en|Nishi-ku, Sakai|WD=Q1042499|lt=Nishi-ku, Sakai|1=Nishi-ku, Sakai}}, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture. It is situated in the southern part of the {{ill|Mozu Tombs|en|Mozu Tombs|WD=Q4250584|lt=Mozu Tombs|1=Mozu Tombs}} and is one of the largest tumuli in Japan, with a mound length of 365 meters. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} has designated it as the "'''Mozu Mimihara Minami Mausoleum'''" (百舌鳥耳原南陵), attributing it to {{ill|Emperor Richū|en|Emperor Richū|WD=Q329704|lt=Emperor Richu|1=Emperor Richū}}, the 17th emperor of Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Cultural heritage|en|Cultural heritage|WD=Q210272|lt=World Cultural Heritage|1=Cultural heritage}} site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."
== Overview == The name "Misanzai" is thought to be a phonetic variation of the term "misasagi" (陵), meaning imperial mausoleum.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://crd.ndl.go.jp/reference/modules/d3ndlcrdentry/index.php?page=ref_view&id=1000261206|title=Etymology of Kofun Names|publisher=National Diet Library|accessdate=2021-11-19}}</ref> Historical maps from the Edo period refer to the site as "Richu Tennō-ryō" (履中天皇陵). Prior to being incorporated into Sakai City, the area was part of {{ill|Shinseki Village|ja|神石村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinseki Village|1=Kamiishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590694}}, Senboku District.
Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} constructed on the western edge of a plateau, with the front mound facing south. Its placement and orientation were likely chosen to make the tumulus visible from Osaka Bay.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/911/00000153|title=Richu Tennō-ryō Kofun / Component Asset / World Heritage|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-22}}</ref>
The mound consists of three tiers and features protrusions (造り出し) on both sides of the constricted area, although the eastern protrusion is now significantly eroded.<ref name=":0" /> The total length of the tumulus is 365 meters, making it the third-largest in Japan after {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Emperor Nintoku's mausoleum) and {{ill|Honmyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honmyōyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Ōjin's mausoleum). The rear mound has a diameter of 205 meters and a height of 27.6 meters, while the front mound measures 235 meters in width and 25.3 meters in height.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html|title=Ranking of Kofun Sizes in Japan|publisher=Sakai City Cultural and Tourism Bureau|accessdate=2021-11-22}}</ref>
Excavations and aerial surveys have revealed the presence of circular platforms at the tops of the front and rear mounds, a unique feature among keyhole-shaped tumuli. These platforms may have held burial facilities.<ref name=":0" />
The tumulus is surrounded by a large moat, with a width of approximately 62 meters at the front mound and 70 meters at the rear mound. While the current moat is single-layered, earlier surveys suggest it originally had an outer moat, possibly a dry moat.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster Research Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|date=2008}}</ref>
Artifacts recovered from the site include a variety of haniwa, such as house-shaped and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as Sue pottery. The presence of a massive quiver-shaped haniwa, standing 1.4 meters tall, suggests the tumulus was constructed in the early 5th century.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster Research Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|date=2008}}</ref>
== Satellite Tumuli == Several smaller tumuli, believed to be satellite tombs (陪塚), surround the main tumulus. Of these, only the {{ill|Shichikannon Kofun|ja|七観音古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichikannon Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Terayama Minamiyama Kofun|ja|寺山南山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terayama Minamiyama Kofun|qq=}} remain intact today. Historical records suggest that other satellite tombs, such as the {{ill|Nanakunoyama Kofun|lt=Nanakunoyama Kofun|lt=Shichikanyama Kofun|ja|七観山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichikanyama Kofun|qq=}}, once existed and were contemporaneous with the main tumulus.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster Research Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|date=2008}}</ref>
== World Heritage == In 2019, Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}}</ref>
== Access == * **JR Hanwa Line**: A 6-minute walk (500 meters) from {{ill|Uenoshiba Station|en|Uenoshiba Station|WD=Q856750|lt=Uenoshiba Station|1=Uenoshiba Station}} to the Richu Tennō Mausoleum. * **Nankai Koya Line**: From Sakaihigashi Station, take a bus to "Nankyo-dori Yoncho" (南陵通四丁) and walk 5 minutes.

== Satellite Tumuli == Several smaller tumuli, believed to be satellite tombs (陪塚), surround the main tumulus. Of these, only the {{ill|Shichikannon Kofun|ja|七観音古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichikannon Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Terayama Minamiyama Kofun|ja|寺山南山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terayama Minamiyama Kofun|qq=}} remain intact today. Historical records suggest that other satellite tombs, such as the {{ill|Nanakunoyama Kofun|lt=Nanakunoyama Kofun|lt=Shichikanyama Kofun|ja|七観山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichikanyama Kofun|qq=}}, once existed and were contemporaneous with the main tumulus.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster Research Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|date=2008}}</ref>
== World Heritage == In 2019, Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}}</ref>
== Access == * **JR Hanwa Line**: A 6-minute walk (500 meters) from {{ill|Uenoshiba Station|en|Uenoshiba Station|qid=Q856750|lt=Uenoshiba Station|1=Uenoshiba Station}} to the Richu Tennō Mausoleum. * **Nankai Koya Line**: From Sakaihigashi Station, take a bus to "Nankyo-dori Yoncho" (南陵通四丁) and walk 5 minutes.
== References == {{reflist}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun]][[ja:上石津ミサンザイ古墳]][[zh:上石津御陵古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun]][[ja:上石津ミサンザイ古墳]][[zh:上石津御陵古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun]][[ja:上石津ミサンザイ古墳]][[zh:上石津御陵古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上石津ミサンザイ古墳]][[zh:上石津御陵古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun zenkei-2.JPG|location={{ill|Nishi-ku|qid=Q1042499|1=Nishi-ku, Sakai}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.5539|N|135.4775|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}| Interred_individual = {{ill|Richū|qid=Q329704|1=Emperor Richū}}|part_of={{ill|Mozu Tombs|qid=Q4250584|1=Mozu Tombs}}}}
[[File:Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun zenkei-2.JPG|280px|thumb|center|Full view of the tumulus (foreground: front mound, background: rear mound)]] '''Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun''' (上石津ミサンザイ古墳), also known as the '''Ishizugaoka Kofun''' (石津ヶ丘古墳), is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|en|Nishi-ku, Sakai|qid=Q1042499|lt=Nishi-ku, Sakai|1=Nishi-ku, Sakai}}, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture. It is situated in the southern part of the {{ill|Mozu Tombs|en|Mozu Tombs|qid=Q4250584|lt=Mozu Tombs|1=Mozu Tombs}} and is one of the largest tumuli in Japan, with a mound length of 365 meters. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} has designated it as the "'''Mozu Mimihara Minami Mausoleum'''" (百舌鳥耳原南陵), attributing it to {{ill|Emperor Richū|en|Emperor Richū|qid=Q329704|lt=Emperor Richu|1=Emperor Richū}}, the 17th emperor of Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Cultural heritage|en|Cultural heritage|qid=Q210272|lt=World Cultural Heritage|1=Cultural heritage}} site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."
== Overview == The name "Misanzai" is thought to be a phonetic variation of the term "misasagi" (陵), meaning imperial mausoleum.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://crd.ndl.go.jp/reference/modules/d3ndlcrdentry/index.php?page=ref_view&id=1000261206|title=Etymology of Kofun Names|publisher=National Diet Library|accessdate=2021-11-19}}</ref> Historical maps from the Edo period refer to the site as "Richu Tennō-ryō" (履中天皇陵). Prior to being incorporated into Sakai City, the area was part of {{ill|Shinseki Village|ja|神石村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinseki Village|1=Kamiishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11590694}}, Senboku District.
Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} constructed on the western edge of a plateau, with the front mound facing south. Its placement and orientation were likely chosen to make the tumulus visible from Osaka Bay.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/911/00000153|title=Richu Tennō-ryō Kofun / Component Asset / World Heritage|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-22}}</ref>
The mound consists of three tiers and features protrusions (造り出し) on both sides of the constricted area, although the eastern protrusion is now significantly eroded.<ref name=":0" /> The total length of the tumulus is 365 meters, making it the third-largest in Japan after {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Emperor Nintoku's mausoleum) and {{ill|Honmyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honmyōyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Ōjin's mausoleum). The rear mound has a diameter of 205 meters and a height of 27.6 meters, while the front mound measures 235 meters in width and 25.3 meters in height.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html|title=Ranking of Kofun Sizes in Japan|publisher=Sakai City Cultural and Tourism Bureau|accessdate=2021-11-22}}</ref>
Excavations and aerial surveys have revealed the presence of circular platforms at the tops of the front and rear mounds, a unique feature among keyhole-shaped tumuli. These platforms may have held burial facilities.<ref name=":0" />
The tumulus is surrounded by a large moat, with a width of approximately 62 meters at the front mound and 70 meters at the rear mound. While the current moat is single-layered, earlier surveys suggest it originally had an outer moat, possibly a dry moat.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster Research Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|date=2008}}</ref>
Artifacts recovered from the site include a variety of haniwa, such as house-shaped and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as Sue pottery. The presence of a massive quiver-shaped haniwa, standing 1.4 meters tall, suggests the tumulus was constructed in the early 5th century.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster Research Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|date=2008}}</ref>
== Satellite Tumuli == Several smaller tumuli, believed to be satellite tombs (陪塚), surround the main tumulus. Of these, only the {{ill|Shichikannon Kofun|ja|七観音古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichikannon Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Terayama Minamiyama Kofun|ja|寺山南山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terayama Minamiyama Kofun|qq=}} remain intact today. Historical records suggest that other satellite tombs, such as the {{ill|Nanakunoyama Kofun|lt=Nanakunoyama Kofun|lt=Shichikanyama Kofun|ja|七観山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichikanyama Kofun|qq=}}, once existed and were contemporaneous with the main tumulus.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster Research Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|date=2008}}</ref>
== World Heritage == In 2019, Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}}</ref>
== Access == * **JR Hanwa Line**: A 6-minute walk (500 meters) from {{ill|Uenoshiba Station|en|Uenoshiba Station|qid=Q856750|lt=Uenoshiba Station|1=Uenoshiba Station}} to the Richu Tennō Mausoleum. * **Nankai Koya Line**: From Sakaihigashi Station, take a bus to "Nankyo-dori Yoncho" (南陵通四丁) and walk 5 minutes.
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/017/index.html Mozu Mimihara Minami Mausoleum - Imperial Household Agency] * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/richutenno.html Richu Tennō-ryō Kofun - Sakai City Official Website] * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/index.html Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - World Heritage Site - Sakai City] {{translated page|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|version=103389543|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Prior to being incorporated into Sakai City, the area was part of {{ill|Shinseki Village|ja|神石村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinseki Village|1=Kamiishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11590694}}, Senboku District.
Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}} constructed on the western edge of a plateau, with the front mound facing south. Its placement and orientation were likely chosen to make the tumulus visible from Osaka Bay.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/911/00000153|title=Richu Tennō-ryō Kofun / Component Asset / World Heritage|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-22}}</ref>
The mound consists of three tiers and features protrusions (造り出し) on both sides of the constricted area, although the eastern protrusion is now significantly eroded.<ref name=":0" /> The total length of the tumulus is 365 meters, making it the third-largest in Japan after {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Emperor Nintoku's mausoleum) and {{ill|Honmyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honmyōyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Ōjin's mausoleum). The rear mound has a diameter of 205 meters and a height of 27.6 meters, while the front mound measures 235 meters in width and 25.3 meters in height.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html|title=Ranking of Kofun Sizes in Japan|publisher=Sakai City Cultural and Tourism Bureau|accessdate=2021-11-22}}</ref>
Excavations and aerial surveys have revealed the presence of circular platforms at the tops of the front and rear mounds, a unique feature among keyhole-shaped tumuli. These platforms may have held burial facilities.<ref name=":0" />
The tumulus is surrounded by a large moat, with a width of approximately 62 meters at the front mound and 70 meters at the rear mound. While the current moat is single-layered, earlier surveys suggest it originally had an outer moat, possibly a dry moat.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster Research Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|date=2008}}</ref>
Artifacts recovered from the site include a variety of haniwa, such as house-shaped and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as Sue pottery. The presence of a massive quiver-shaped haniwa, standing 1.4 meters tall, suggests the tumulus was constructed in the early 5th century.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster Research Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|date=2008}}</ref>
== Satellite Tumuli == Several smaller tumuli, believed to be satellite tombs (陪塚), surround the main tumulus. Of these, only the {{ill|Shichikannon Kofun|ja|七観音古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichikannon Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Terayama Minamiyama Kofun|ja|寺山南山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terayama Minamiyama Kofun|qq=}} remain intact today. Historical records suggest that other satellite tombs, such as the {{ill|Nanakunoyama Kofun|lt=Nanakunoyama Kofun|lt=Shichikanyama Kofun|ja|七観山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichikanyama Kofun|qq=}}, once existed and were contemporaneous with the main tumulus.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster Research Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|date=2008}}</ref>
== World Heritage == In 2019, Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}}</ref>
== Access == * **JR Hanwa Line**: A 6-minute walk (500 meters) from {{ill|Uenoshiba Station|en|Uenoshiba Station|qid=Q856750|lt=Uenoshiba Station|1=Uenoshiba Station}} to the Richu Tennō Mausoleum. * **Nankai Koya Line**: From Sakaihigashi Station, take a bus to "Nankyo-dori Yoncho" (南陵通四丁) and walk 5 minutes.
== References == {{reflist}}
